text: Imagining a future action with sensory specificity — picturing where you will be, what you will see, how your hands will move — significantly increases the probability of carrying out that action. This is not merely motivational; it is structural. Detailed mental simulation appears to preactivate the same motor and perceptual circuits that will later execute the act, creating a grooved neural path. A study by Richard Wiseman found that people who imagined the route to a polling station, not just their intention to vote, showed markedly higher actual turnout. The simulation functions as a rehearsal, and rehearsal is one of memory's most reliable encoding strategies.
question: According to the passage, why does sensory-specific mental simulation increase follow-through?

options.text: It strengthens declarative memory for the goal itself.
It preactivates the motor and perceptual circuits that will execute the act.
It converts time-based intentions into event-based ones.
It reduces anxiety associated with the intended action.
